vulnerability.title: Configure Logind to terminate idle sessions after certain time of inactivity
vulnerability.description: To configure logind service to terminate inactive user sessions after seconds, edit the file /etc/systemd/logind.conf. Ensure that there is a section [Login] which contains the configuration StopIdleSessionSec= .
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { ( grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?rhel[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="8.7"; printf "%s\n%s" "$expected" "$real" | sort -VC; } && grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?rhel[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="9.0"; [[ "$real" != "$expected" ]]; } ) || grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?ol[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="8.7"; printf "%s\n%s" "$expected" "$real"

triage_decision.reason: The setting configures session timeout for logind, a standard STIG hardening control; remediation involves editing a config file and reloading systemd, which does not risk system breakage.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; May cause user session termination if timeout is too aggressive; Requires service reload; misconfiguration could affect all local login sessions

# Defaults can be restored by simply deleting this file and all drop-ins.
#
# Use 'systemd-analyze cat-config systemd/logind.conf' to display the full config.
#
# See logind.conf(5) for details.

[Login]
#NAutoVTs=6
#ReserveVT=6
#KillUserProcesses=no
#KillOnlyUsers=
#KillExcludeUsers=root
#InhibitDelayMaxSec=5
#UserStopDelaySec=10
#HandlePowerKey=poweroff
#HandlePowerKeyLongPress=ignore
#HandleRebootKey=reboot
#HandleRebootKeyLongPress=poweroff
#HandleSuspendKey=suspend
#HandleSuspendKeyLongPress=hibernate
#HandleHibernateKey=hibernate
#HandleHibernateKeyLongPress=ignore
#HandleLidSwitch=suspend
#HandleLidSwitchExternalPower=suspend
#HandleLidSwitchDocked=ignore
#PowerKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#SuspendKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#HibernateKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#LidSwitchIgnoreInhibited=yes
#RebootKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#HoldoffTimeoutSec=30s
#IdleAction=ignore
#IdleActionSec=30min
#RuntimeDirectorySize=10%
#RuntimeDirectoryInodesMax=
#RemoveIPC=no
#InhibitorsMax=8192
#SessionsMax=8192

review_feedback: Review+QA approved but the verification scan failed. Try a different approach.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The fix correctly adds StopIdleSessionSec to /etc/systemd/logind.conf and reloads systemd, thereby resolving the vulnerability, but it lacks safeguards such as OS version checks, timeout validation, and idempotent handling.
concerns: May cause abrupt termination of idle user sessions, potentially leading to data loss.
Does not validate OS version or logind.conf syntax before modification.
Does not verify or sanitize the timeout value format.
Potentially creates duplicate entries if the directive already exists with comments.
No rollback mechanism or verification step included.
suggested_improvements: Add a check for the presence of the [Login] section and create it if missing.
Validate the OS version and logind.conf syntax before applying changes.
Sanitize or parameterize the timeout value to ensure it matches supported formats.
Implement idempotent updates to avoid duplicate lines.
Include a rollback step and verify the new setting with systemctl show.
